mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the lower bound regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% as of 2016-07-01 indicates post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is triggered when employment growth crosses the 150k threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,448k as of 2016-07-01 indicates near-full employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline together, indicating broad-based demand weakness rather than sector-specific shocks.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,189k and RSAFS at 457,727M as of 2016-06-01 show stable demand
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand shock
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) produces no statistically significant additional predictive power beyond single-series models (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and margin compression that reduces forward equity returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism is always-on and operates through the real-time signal of production momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.13 as of 2016-06-01 shows modest production levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or negative 5-day forward returns occur in < 52% of observations when INDPRO growth < 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises above 5.0% in a single month, signaling a deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is threshold-triggered and reflects market repricing of growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-07-01 is near full employment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200k units, signals strong demand-side momentum that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The interaction of strong growth and housing strength indicates broad-based expansion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND HOUST > 1,200k units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,575.1B and HOUST at 1,189k indicate near-trend growth
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with housing recovery
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no significant explanatory power beyond additive single-series effects (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 2.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 0.25%, creating a real rate tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to identify monetary policy tightness relative to inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.93 and FEDFUNDS at 0.39% indicate low inflation with near-zero rates
regime_hint: low-inflation monetary policy regime with policy normalization
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 53% of triggered instances, or the real rate tightening signal produces no incremental predictive power beyond nominal rate alone (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, signaling a supply shock combined with demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. The joint deterioration indicates stagflationary pressure or recession onset.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.13 and UNRATE at 4.9% show stable conditions as of mid-2016
regime_hint: supply shock or early recession regime
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces no additional predictive power beyond the individual series (p > 0.10).
